title: Nephrodium marginatum
taxonomy: Nephrodium marginatum is an extinct species of fern, and its classification is a part of the broader fern classification system. It is often classified within the family Matoniaaceae or the family Nephrodiumaceae. However, some sources may group it under the order Cyatheales. The specific classification may vary depending on the source and the current understanding of fern taxonomy. Further research is needed to determine the most accurate classification for Nephrodium marginatum.
uses: Nephrodium marginatum, also known as the Marginated Lycopodium or commonly as the Black Beaked Lycopodium, was a species of plant with potential medicinal properties.
